description: The Berlin Senate enacted the 'Mietendeckel' (rent cap) in 2019 to freeze rents for five years to combat housing affordability. The policy faced intense legal battles, eventually being declared unconstitutional by the Federal Constitutional Court in April 2021, favoring property developers and landlords.

key_events.description: Berlin Senate announces the Mietendeckel legislation.
Major landlords initiate legal challenges against the city.
Federal Constitutional Court rules the law unconstitutional.

calibration_notes: Useful for measuring how populist policy momentum can be eroded by judicial intervention.
